questions.question: Define a constructor in Java and explain its purpose.
keyPoints: A constructor has the same name as the class and no return type.
Its primary purpose is to initialize the newly created object with a valid state.

questions.question: Explain constructor overloading with an example.
keyPoints: Constructor overloading allows multiple constructors with different parameter lists.
Java selects the appropriate constructor based on the number and types of arguments passed.
Provide an example demonstrating constructor overloading with at least two constructors.

questions.question: Discuss the concept of constructor chaining with this() and its advantages.
keyPoints: Constructor chaining allows one constructor to call another constructor using the this() keyword.
The this() keyword must be the first statement in a constructor.
Explain the benefits of constructor chaining, such as reducing code duplication and improving constructor organization.

questions.question: Compare and contrast constructors and methods in Java.
keyPoints: Constructors are special methods called automatically when an object is created.
Methods are used to perform actions on an object.
Constructors have no return type, while methods may have a return type.
Provide a table or diagram highlighting the key differences between constructors and methods.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a default constructor in Java.
keyPoints: A default constructor is provided by Java if no other constructor is defined.
The default constructor is a no-argument constructor that does nothing.
